libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. Generated tests should target the 2018 API surface: use SchemaParser.parse(Reader), parse(String), or parse(File), and avoid newer 19.x-only helpers such as PropertyDataFetcher.setUseSetAccessible, PropertyDataFetcher.setUseNegativeCache, and DataFetchingEnvironmentImpl.newDataFetchingEnvironment().

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7995 [Automation] javac compile fails for com.graphql-java:graphql-java:2018-07-30T00-23-12-bfee885; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 19.7 test sources exercise newer APIs and will not compile unchanged against 2018-07-30T00-23-12-bfee885.
Do not carry over the 19.7 relocated-Guava workaround unless local verification proves it is needed; the 2018 artifact POM and bytecode inspection did not show Guava/relocated-Guava references.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The 2018 graphql-java artifact declares only normal compile-time Maven dependencies that Gradle will resolve transitively, and core GraphQL usage does not require Docker, external services, extra test dependencies, or environment/system properties.
